data.title: JP Morgan initiates coverage on cement sector amid consolidation, recovering demand
data.description:
Scorching heat, labour shortages in some regions further dented demand during Q2FY25.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Global broking firm JP Morgan initiated coverage on the domestic cement sector, as the segment looks set for consolidation, with larger players turning aggressive.

According to the brokerage, the urbanisation and infrastructure projects should also boost medium-term demand for the companies. Capacity utilisation shall remain stable, despite the further capacity expansion.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Overall, the cement industry should see some recovery from the recent weakness seen in the sector, as government capex grew around 40 percent on-year following the monsoon. Additionally, companies cutting their costs shall bode well for the industry.

JP Morgan has initiated coverage on several cement companies, issuing an "overweight" rating for UltraTech Cement and ACC with target prices of Rs 13,750 and Rs 3,020 per share, respectively.

The broking firm issued a "neutral" call to Ambuja Cements and Shree Cements, setting targets at Rs 590 and Rs 25,175 per share.

Meanwhile, Dalmia Bharat received an "underweight" call, with a target price of Rs 1,550 per share, as the brokerage noted concerns over Dalmia Bharat's ability to raise utilisation.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live updates

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Cement companies have been plagued with woes over the recent quarter. The delayed infrastructure activities following the general election, unavailability of sand, above-average monsoon rains, and flooding in various regions across the country adversely impacted cement sales. Scorching heat, labour shortages in some regions further dented demand during Q2FY25.

Pricing remained under pressure across regions. While multiple attempts were made by cement manufacturers to hike prices during the quarter, monsoon, floods, and lack of infrastructure activities led to hikes being rolled back, keeping cement realizations under pressure.

data.title: HDFC Bank Q2 results impresses Street, brokerages forecast up to 28% upside on falling CD ratio
data.description:
So far this year, shares of HDFC Bank have dropped over a percent

The Street gave HDFC Bank's September quarter performance a thumbs up owing to strong metrics on all counts, with slight deterioation in its asset quality picture. Brokerages maintained their positive outlook on the stock, with price targets suggesting potential upside of up to 28 percent from current levels. The consensus view is that the bank is on a stable path to address post-merger challenges as NII, profit metrics grow and credit to deposit ratio drops.

Bernstein reaffirmed its 'outperform' rating, setting a target price of Rs 2,100 per share. The firm noted that HDFC Bank appeared resilient compared to its peers, which have struggled with shrinking margins and rising credit costs. According to Bernstein, HDFC Bank's robust fundamentals, particularly in a challenging environment, differentiate it from the rest of the sector.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Goldman Sachs echoed this bullish sentiment, reiterating its 'buy' rating and offering the highest target price among brokerages at Rs 2,156 per share. Analysts said that HDFC Bank's core operating profits were in line with expectations, but it saw an improved outlook for future earnings.

ALSO READ: HDFC Bank to sell up to Rs 10,000-crore shares in HDB Financial IPO; total public issue size at Rs 12,500 crore

"The core PPoP RoA of 2.7 percent marked a second consecutive quarter with improvement. While provisions were 10 percent lower than estimates, PAT beat Street estimates by 10 percent," they added.

HDFC Bank's key financial metrics showed solid growth, with 6 percent year-on-year rise in September quarter net profit, while net interest income grew by 10 percent YoY. Its NIMs also stood at 3.46 percent in Q2FY25, in-line with Street estimates.

The bank’s asset quality deteriorated, with gross non-performing assets (NPAs) at 1.36 percent in Q2FY25, compared to 1.33 per cent in Q1FY25, and 1.34 percent in Q2FY24. Net NPAs of the bank stood at 0.41 percent compared to 0.39 percent in Q1FY25.

A notable highlight from the quarter was a sharp drop in the bank's credit-to-deposit (CD) ratio, which fell to 99.8 percent. This marked the first time the ratio has dropped below 100 percent since the merger with mortgage giant HDFC in July 2023. Without giving a timeline or a desired level, the management said it would like to bring down the level further.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"It will take another 2-3 years to get the CD ratio back to the pre-merger levels of 86-87 percent," said S Vaidyanathan, Chief Financial Officer of HDFC Bank on a concall post Q2 results release.

In response to the latest results, Investec raised its target price for HDFC Bank to Rs 1,625 per share from Rs 1,650 per share. However, the firm maintained a 'neutral' rating, pointing out that the bank's slower credit growth could limit further reductions in the CD ratio. They also highlighted that CASA (current account savings account) growth remains a critical factor in driving profitability.

data.title: Brokerages bullish as Zomato considers $1 billion QIP, 49% FII limit
data.description:
According to the September shareholding pattern, through institutional holdings and FDIs, foreign investors hold around 52.5 percent.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Food-aggregator Zomato has been the apple of investors' eyes ever since the food aggregator's quick-commerce model showed promise. With Zomato announcing a qualified institutional placement (QIP) to raise funds despite having $1.2 billion in its books, brokerages reiterated their bullishness on the food aggregator.

On October 18, Zomato announced its board would consider fundraising via the QIP route on October 22. While the amount remained undisclosed, reports suggest it could be to the tune of Rs 8,500 crore or $1 billion. If approved, this will be the first fundraise by the Gurugram-headquartered food delivery giant since its stock market debut in 2021.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Follow our market blog to catch all the live updates

Further, reports suggest that Zomato would apply to the Reserve Bank of India to limit its foreign institutional holding to 49 percent. According to the September shareholding pattern, through institutional holdings and FDIs, foreign investors hold around 52.5 percent in Zomato.

International brokerage Jefferies reiterated its bullish buy call on Zomato, with a target price of Rs 335. If the RBI approves Zomato's application on the FII holding front, the move will convert Zomato to a 'domestic' firm. This could potentially result in Zomato's stock being deleted from the MSCI, said the brokerage. However, the process of implementing the FII cap will take time.

The key reason to take such a step would be to convert to a 1P model, which improves profitability. Foreign firms cannot run an inventory led, 1P model and need to restrict operations to marketplace, noted the brokerage.

Currently, according to Blinkit's model, local third party entrepreneurs own the inventory and sell it to users on Blinkit platform, where riders deliver goods to the buyers. However, if Zomato cuts its FII shareholding limit, Blinkit can move to a 1P model, which will allow it to follow the inventory model.

The take-rate for vendors is around 2 percent of the total gross order value, to compensate for running the operations & earning return. On the other hand, the inventory model will allow Blinkit to have a tighter control over the inventory and take calculated risks when it comes to launching or scaling up new categories, as it expands well-beyond groceries, said Jefferies.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Bank of America also maintained its buy rating on Zomato, with a target price of Rs 325 per share. The brokerage sees the quick commerce industry going through a phase of increased competition within the next 6-12 months.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Waaree Energies IPO to open shortly for public subscription: Key things to know before applying
data.description:
Waaree Energies' subscription for Rs 4,321.44 crore-IPO will close on October 23

Waaree Energies IPO is set to hit the primary market shortly today for its Rs 4,000-crore maiden share sale. The company is a major player in the solar energy industry focused on PV module manufacturing with an aggregate installed capacity of 12 GW as of June 30, 2023.

There is also much buzz around the Waaree Energies grey market premium that has crossed the 100 percent premium ahead of its initial share sale on October 21. This comes after sluggish retail demand for the biggest-ever IPO of Hyundai Motor India, which concluded last week with a 237 percent subscription. The demand had remained subdued, impacted by market volatility and a weakened outlook for the auto sector.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Waaree Energies IPO GMP soars over 85% as company fixes price band

Waaree Energies IPO: Key things to know before issue opens today

1) Waaree Energies IPO GMP: According to various platforms that track the grey market activities, the GMP for Waaree Energies IPO has soared in the price range of Rs 1,500 - 1,510 in the unregulated market, reflecting the high demand for the company's shares. The high GMP indicates listing gains of over 100 percent. This substantial premium in the grey market indicates that investors are willing to pay a significant premium over the IPO's issue price to secure a stake in Waaree Energies.

2) Waaree Energies IPO Price Band: Solar panel maker has fixed a price band of Rs 1,427 to Rs 1,503 per share for its IPO.

3) Waaree Energies IPO Dates: IPO will open for public subscription today at 10 am and conclude on October 23 at 5 PM.

4) Waaree Energies IPO Size: The IPO is a combination of a fresh issue of equity shares aggregating to Rs 3,600 crore and an offer-for-sale (OFS) of 48 lakh equity shares worth Rs 721.44 crore, at the upper end of the price band, by a promoter and existing shareholders. This translates into the total issue size of Rs 4,321.44 crore.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

5) IPO details: Under the OFS, promoter Waaree Sustainable Finance Pvt Ltd and shareholder Chandurkar Investments Private Ltd are offloading shares.

6) IPO Objective: Proceeds from the fresh issue will be used for setting up the 6 GW of Ingot Wafer, Solar Cell and Solar PV module manufacturing facility in Odisha. Besides, a portion will be used for general corporate purposes.

7) Market Valuation: The company's market valuation has been pegged at over Rs 4,300 crore post-issue at the upper end of the price band.

8) Reserved lots: Half of the issue size has been reserved for qualified institutional investors, 35 percent for retail investors and the remaining 15 percent for non-institutional investors. shares valued Rs 65 crore have been reserved for the employees of the solar panel maker.

9) The company operates five manufacturing facilities with one factory each, located at Surat, Tumb, Nandigram and Chikhli in Gujarat, and the IndoSolar Facility, in Noida, Uttar Pradesh.

10) Brokerage notes: KR Choksey has a 'subscribe' call on the IPO, saying "at a P/E ratio of 33.9x, Waaree offers an attractive investment opportunity in India’s booming renewable energy market. Based on these factors, we recommend a “subscribe” rating for the upcoming IPO." Monarch Networth Capital also has a 'Subscribe' rating to the issue.

The shares of the company will list on October 28 and the registrar to the issue is Link Intime India Private Limited.

The October primary market has remained abuzz with public issues garnering massive response to issues. Hyundai Motor India which concluded its sale last week was subscribed 2.37x as the institutional demand helps Hyundai cross the IPO line.

data.title: Gen AI and the nuclear-powered future
data.description:
Big AI’s push for nuclear power has also changed the global negotiations on climate change

Generative AI companies like Open AI, Google and others have come to realise one thing. Even a rapid scaling up of conventional energy as well as renewable energy capacities may not be enough to feed the insatiable power appetite of Large Language Models (LLMs). The Tech Bros are now putting their bets on nuclear power – and looking at not only restarting mothballed nuclear power plants but also building new plants. They are also investing in new

data.title: Business News Live: IPO subscriptions for Waaree Energies, Deepak Builders & Engineers to open today
data.description:
Business News Live: Three major initial public offerings (IPOs) - Waaree Energies, Premium Plast, Deepak Builders & Engineers- will open for subscription today (October 21), while Freshara Agro Exports' issue will close.

Waaree Energies' subscription for Rs 4,321.44 crore-IPO, which consists of a fresh issue worth Rs 3,600 crore and an offer-for-sale worth Rs 721.44 crore, will close on October 23, with the allotment likely on October 24 and listing on October 28.

Premium Plast and Deepak Builders & Engineers are likely to follow Waaree's plan.

The subscription for Rs 75.39-crore Freshara Agro Exports IPO will close today with allotment expected to be finalized on October 22 and listing on October 24.

Meanwhile, India's biggest issue Hyundai Motor India IPO is set for listing on October 22.

data.title: Brokerages slash target prices on Tata Consumer as Q2 show disappoints
data.description:
The key focus for Tata Consumer needs to be maintaining its market share, instead of margins, said Morgan Stanley.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Analysts slashed their target price on Tetley Tea maker Tata Consumer Products after the FMCG player reported a muted show for its July-September earnings scorecard.

The the owner of Ching's Secret noodles brands said its net profit was unchanged at Rs 359 crore. Revenue increased 13 percent to Rs 4,214 crore in the three months ended September 30, as compared to the same period last year at Rs 3,734 crore, Tata Consumer said in an exchange filing on October 18.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

According to the company, erratic weather patterns have affected tea and salt production in India, leading to input cost inflation. Therefore India beverages had a soft quarter, revenue for the quarter declined 3 percent, with volume declining 4 percent owing to soft demand.

Raw tea prices saw significant inflation during the quarter. Staggered price increases have been actioned across brands. Coffee continued its strong trajectory and grew 29 percent during the quarter.

Also Read | Tata Consumer Q2 net profit unchanged at Rs 359 crore

International brokerage Jefferies maintained its 'hold' call, but cut its target price to Rs 1,170, down from Rs 1,190 earlier. The brokerage said Tata Consumer saw another weak quarter, especially in the India business, as both, revenues and margins missed its estimates.

Like-for-like EBITDA growth crashed 23 percent as a result of surging inflation, especially in input costs and raw margins in the tea business. The tea business also reported decling volumes, with NourishCo seeing a modest trend. The top performer across segments was the Tata Salt business.

The sole bright spot in Tata Consumer's Q2 show were the acquisitions, that had a higher than expected share in revenue contribution.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Morgan Stanley also cut its target price on the player from Rs 1,344 per share to Rs 1,273. However, the brokerage maintained its 'overweight' rating, despite saying that there was a miss across the domestic business.

The brokerage noted that organic business revenue growth was weak at fiver percent, with  a volume decline in tea. The growth businesses' organic growth was weaker at 15 percent. However, the non-branded and the international segments continued to do well.

The key focus for Tata Consumer needs to be maintaining its market share, instead of margins, said Morgan Stanley, especially since the demand in the rural segment is recovering gradually, while urban demand has softened.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.
